item.title: AWS credential validation exposes full access key ID in account field — should be masked
item.intent: AWS credentials are never logged or displayed unmasked. Access key in account field shows first 8 chars only. Whitespace-only tokens are rejected.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/providers/aws.ts:223 returns { valid: true, account: accessKeyId } with the full AWS access key ID unmasked. AWS key IDs (AKIA...) are 20 chars and considered sensitive in logs and status displays. Additionally, !accessKeyId passes for whitespace-only strings. Steps: (1) trim both accessKeyId and secretKey before all use and checks; (2) reject if trimmed value is empty; (3) mask account field as first 8 chars plus ... (e.g. AKIAIOSFOD...); (4) add test for whitespace-only key, verify masking. Scope: ~6 lines in aws.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Whitespace-only access key is rejected with the same format error as empty key
account field in successful validation shows first 8 chars plus ... not the full key ID
Valid credentials still pass validation — no regression
